name: Chili (chili con carne with beans) — food medium
identity_note: FooDB FOOD00707 'Chili' is NOT the chili pepper (Capsicum); it is the prepared Tex-Mex dish chili con carne (beef + kidney beans + tomato + onion + chili pepper + spices), FooDB food group 'Dishes / Tex-Mex'. Therefore animal-derived compounds (cholesterol, B12, saturated/animal fatty acids) are genuine here, not noise. Values below are from USDA SR Legacy NDB 22904 'Chili con carne with beans, canned entree' (247 kcal / 242 g cup), rescaled to per 100 g (divide serving values by 2.42).

components.name: starch
components.evidence: 16.70 g / 242 g; dominant carbohydrate, from beans (hydrolyzed to glucose on utilization)

components.name: citric acid
components.source: inferred from tomato ingredient; not resolved in USDA SR 22904
components.evidence: Tomato (a defining ingredient) is a citrate-dominant matrix (~0.2-0.4 g citric/100g tomato); present in the dish but USDA composite does not quantify organic acids. Growth-relevant carbon source, flagged for quantification.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: inferred from tomato/onion ingredients; not resolved in USDA SR 22904
components.evidence: Secondary organic acid of tomato; present but unquantified in the composite.

components.name: L-glutamate (glutamic acid)
components.source: present via 5.8 g/100g beef+kidney-bean protein; USDA SR 22904 lacks per-amino-acid data
components.evidence: Most abundant residue in both beef and bean protein; growth-relevant N/C source.

components.name: L-aspartate (aspartic acid)
components.source: present via mixed protein; USDA SR 22904 lacks per-amino-acid data

components.name: L-arginine
components.source: present via mixed protein; USDA SR 22904 lacks per-amino-acid data

components.name: L-lysine
components.source: present via mixed protein; USDA SR 22904 lacks per-amino-acid data
components.evidence: Rich in both beef and beans.

components.name: L-leucine
components.source: present via mixed protein; USDA SR 22904 lacks per-amino-acid data

components.name: L-isoleucine
components.source: present via mixed protein; USDA SR 22904 lacks per-amino-acid data

components.name: L-valine
components.source: present via mixed protein; USDA SR 22904 lacks per-amino-acid data
components.evidence: Standard proteinogenic residue (was missing from current list).

components.name: L-threonine
components.source: present via mixed protein; USDA SR 22904 lacks per-amino-acid data

components.name: L-phenylalanine
components.source: present via mixed protein; USDA SR 22904 lacks per-amino-acid data
components.evidence: Standard proteinogenic residue (was missing from current list).

components.name: L-tyrosine
components.source: present via mixed protein; USDA SR 22904 lacks per-amino-acid data

components.name: L-tryptophan
components.source: present via mixed protein; USDA SR 22904 lacks per-amino-acid data

components.name: L-methionine
components.source: present via mixed protein; USDA SR 22904 lacks per-amino-acid data
components.evidence: Limiting AA of the bean fraction.

components.name: L-cystine/L-cysteine
components.source: present via mixed protein; USDA SR 22904 lacks per-amino-acid data

components.name: L-histidine
components.source: present via mixed protein; USDA SR 22904 lacks per-amino-acid data

components.name: L-alanine
components.source: present via mixed protein; USDA SR 22904 lacks per-amino-acid data

components.name: L-proline
components.source: present via mixed protein; USDA SR 22904 lacks per-amino-acid data

components.name: L-serine
components.source: present via mixed protein; USDA SR 22904 lacks per-amino-acid data
components.evidence: Proteinogenic L-serine (current record listed 'D-Serine', a model-side artifact).

components.name: glycine
components.source: present via mixed protein; USDA SR 22904 lacks per-amino-acid data

components.name: molybdate (molybdenum)
components.source: essential trace element; not quantified in USDA SR 22904
components.evidence: Present at ultratrace level from legumes; kept as essential cofactor element, unquantified.

components.name: folate (B9)
components.evidence: 50.82 mcg DFE / 242 g (all food folate, 0 folic acid)

components.name: vitamin A (RAE)
components.evidence: 55.66 mcg RAE / 242 g; entirely from provitamin-A carotenoids

components.name: retinol
components.evidence: reported 0.00 mcg; vitamin A is supplied as carotenoids, not preformed retinol

components.name: cholesterol
components.evidence: 21.78 mg / 242 g; genuine (beef), NOT plant-food noise here

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6, LA)
components.evidence: 0.56 g / 242 g; essential FA, dominant PUFA

components.name: chloride
components.source: stoichiometric counter-ion of the high Na (449 mg/100g) added salt
components.evidence: Present as NaCl; kept as a growth-medium ion, magnitude tracks sodium.

components.name: sulfate
components.evidence: Kept as growth-medium sulfur ion; unquantified.

components.name: phosphate
components.evidence: Inorganic + organic phosphate; magnitude from phosphorus.

components.name: ammonium
components.evidence: Kept as growth-medium nitrogen ion; unquantified.

remove.name: Genistein
remove.reason: Soy-specific isoflavone. Chili con carne uses kidney beans (Phaseolus vulgaris), not soy; genistein is not a characteristic measured constituent of this dish. FooDB generic-legume prediction, unquantified.

remove.name: Glycitein
remove.reason: Soy-specific isoflavone; same rationale as genistein. Not characteristic of a kidney-bean/beef dish; FooDB predicted, not measured.

remove.name: Isoflavones (generic)
remove.reason: Umbrella phytoestrogen class carried over from soy; not a defining or measured nutrient of chili con carne. FooDB noise.

remove.name: Gamolenic acid (GLA, 18:3 n-6)
remove.reason: USDA SR 22904 measures gamma-linolenic acid at 0.00 g; GLA is essentially absent from beef/kidney-bean/tomato fat. FooDB artifact.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Ultratrace element at contaminant/non-nutritional level; not quantified as a growth nutrient here. FooDB trace listing.

missing_added: Individual free sugars with amounts: sucrose 1.03, D-fructose 0.49, D-glucose 0.34 g/100g
Starch quantified (6.90 g/100g) — the dominant fermentable carbohydrate
citric acid + malic acid (organic acids from tomato, growth-relevant carbon; present, flagged for quantification)
Full mineral amounts (Na, K, P, Ca, Mg, Fe, Zn, Mn, Cu, Se)
Full B-vitamin amounts + choline, vitamin K, vitamin E, folate, B12
L-valine and L-phenylalanine (proteinogenic amino acids absent from the current list)
keep_uncovered: capsaicin / capsaicinoids (the defining phytochemical of the chili-pepper component; present, no standard BiGG metabolite, unquantified)
delta-tocopherol (d-tocopherol; genuine minor vitamin E vitamer)
lycopene, lutein+zeaxanthin, beta-cryptoxanthin (genuine carotenoids; quantified above but may lack BiGG mets)
sources: USDA SR Legacy NDB 22904 'Chili con carne with beans, canned entree' (247 kcal per 242 g cup) — full proximate/mineral/vitamin/fatty-acid/sugar panel, rescaled to per 100 g
FooDB FOOD00707 (identity: prepared dish 'Chili' = chili con carne, food group Dishes/Tex-Mex)
USDA FoodData Central (fdc.nal.usda.gov) mirror of NDB 22904
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources are STARCH (6.9 g/100g, from beans) plus free sugars (sucrose>fructose>glucose, ~1.9 g/100g total from tomato/onion); substantial mixed beef+bean protein (5.8 g/100g) supplies amino-acid N. This is a MEAT-containing dish, so cholesterol, B12, and animal-type saturated/trans fatty acids are genuine, not FooDB plant-food noise. Two gaps remain unquantified for this specific composite: individual amino acids (USDA SR 22904 does not resolve them) and organic acids (citric/malic from tomato) — both listed with null amounts and flagged. Removed items are soy isoflavones (wrong legume), GLA (measured 0), and ultratrace nickel.
